title: Grasshoppers
content:
Grasshoppers go in many a thumming spring
    And now to stalks of tasseled sow-grass cling,
    That shakes and swees awhile, but still keeps straight;
    While arching oxeye doubles with his weight.
    Next on the cat-tail-grass with farther bound
    He springs, that bends until they touch the ground.
meaning:
This evocative piece by John Clare represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance, inviting the reader to reflect on the deeper themes of human experience and artistic expression.

In the broader context of John Clare's bibliography, these specific lines stand out for their clarity and poignancy. Whether you are searching for inspiration or a moment of quiet contemplation, this work offers a timeless perspective that transcends its original era.

At Linespedia, we have curated this specific poem to ensure it reaches a modern audience. We recommend sharing these lines as a digital poster or using them as a thoughtful caption to connect with others who appreciate the enduring beauty of verse.
